1.description: Effect of KIID regulation on price and return charts in funds' factsheets. This figure displays the development of the use of return charts and price charts by fund companies. We measure the use of the different chart formats in funds' unregulated marketing material (i.e., factsheets). We mark important changes in the regulation of the information presentation on thex-axis: (i) DIR denotes the year of EU Directive 2009/65/EC formulating recommendations for a unified design of return charts in KIIDs and (ii) REG denotes the year when EU Regulation No 583/2010 entered into force making the KIIDs mandatory for all UCITS (i.e., mutual funds based in the European Union). The number of fund companies for which factsheet data were available inMorningstaris displayed. We define the following categories: (i) “prices only” indicates that price charts are available but no return charts were found, (ii) “returns only” indicates that charts of fixed-period returns are available but no price charts were found, (iii) “both formats” indicates that price and return charts are either used together in a company's universal factsheet template or the two chart formats are found in the factsheets of different funds by the respective company. The rest of the fund companies' factsheet templates do not contain any charts on the past development or they contain other chart formats. Panel A displays the equally weighted percentage of fund companies that fall in the respective category. In Panel B, the fund companies are weighted by the assets under management (AUM) of German investors. Data on the fund companies' AUM are collected from yearbooks of theGerman Investment Funds Association(BVI).

4.description: Stimulus return treatment (sample chart). [Color figure can be viewed atwileyonlinelibrary.com]

5.description: Timing and duration of Study 3. This figure displays the development of the DAX over the period of Study 3. The study is conducted in 12 waves. In each wave, subjects had two weeks to respond. The shaded area illustrates the timing and the duration of the individual survey waves. DAX data are from Datastream.

6.description: Main effects of the task and the stimulus. This figure displays the average return expectations (including 95% confidence intervals) across treatments and studies. Expectations in the task price treatments in Studies 1 and 2 are converted to return expectations using the last price level at the time of forecasting. DAX forecasts in the task price treatment in Study 3 are converted to return expectations using the last available DAX daily open level. For responses submitted on a bank holiday, we use the last available DAX daily close level. Market data are from Datastream. [Color figure can be viewed atwileyonlinelibrary.com]

7.description: Distribution of responses in mixed-format treatments. This figure displays the sample distribution of the subjective expectations across the two mixed-format treatments. Each panel displays (i) a smoothed sample distribution of the expectations in Study 1, (ii) a smoothed sample distribution of the expectations in Study 2, and (iii) the simulated distribution of the sample median returns across charts (i.e., chart median). [Color figure can be viewed atwileyonlinelibrary.com]

8.description: Effect of the chart and question format on forecast accuracy. This figure displays the mean absolute errors of the expectations (including 95% confidence intervals) across treatments and studies. The absolute errors in Studies 1 and 2 are calculated based on the mean return (corresponding to the median return) of the respective data-generating process. The mean return for half of the charts equals 1.4% and for the other half equals −1.4%. We use absolute errors as an accuracy measure because in both Studies 1 and 2, subjects are, respectively, asked and incentivized to report the median of their subjective probability distribution. [Color figure can be viewed atwileyonlinelibrary.com]
